slides.title: Motilal Oswal Financial Services Initiation Note
slides.subtitle: Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd | BUY

blocks.content: BUY with SAARTHI Score of 72/100. Motilal Oswal Financial Services represents a compelling investment opportunity driven by its accelerating transformation from a cyclical broking firm into a high-quality, annuity-driven financial services platform. Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) now constitutes 65% of total net revenues — with management guiding for further increases — fundamentally re-rating the quality of the earnings base. CMP of INR 822.90 offers a 37% discount to analyst consensus target of INR 1,127, with a probability-weighted target of INR 1,109.

items: BUY with SAARTHI Score of 72/100. Motilal Oswal Financial Services represents a compelling investment opportunity driven by its accelerating transformation from a cyclical broking
ARR contribution at 65% of total net revenues, reducing cyclical broking volatility
Q2FY25 profits up 123% YoY with 46% revenue growth and 48% ROE
Total AUM at INR 1.77 lakh crore (+46% YoY); 91% of AUM outperforming benchmarks over 3 years
Private Wealth AUM at INR 1.96 lakh crore (+31% YoY); family count up 41% YoY
Long-term credit rating upgraded to AA+ (Stable) by ICRA

slides.title: Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd At A Glance

blocks.content: Motilal Oswal Financial Services, founded in 1987 by Motilal Oswal and Raamdeo Agrawal, has evolved from a research-driven equity broking house into one of India's most comprehensive financial services platforms. The company spans Capital Markets, Asset & Wealth Management, Housing Finance, and Alternative Investments, serving over 1,600,000 customers across 550+ cities through 2,500+ business locations. Research coverage of 300+ companies remains the firm's enduring competitive moat.

Indian Financial Services is a strategic shock absorber for the growth story. India is expected to have 16.57 lakh HNWIs by 2027 and become the fourth-largest private wealth market by 2028, with a 58.4% projected rise in UHNWIs. The financial services sector is projected to nearly double profits by FY30, led by NBFCs growing at 16% annually. Mutual fund penetration at just 5–6% reflects substantial latent growth opportunity. Private credit hit a record USD 9.0 billion in H1 2025, up 53% YoY.

items: Mutual fund penetration at 5-6% — massive latent opportunity
Financial savings shifting from gold/real estate to financial assets
BSE-listed companies grew from 135 (1995) to 5,328 (Feb 2025)
Private wealth set to be 4th-largest globally by 2028
NBFCs growing at 16% annually with improving asset quality
Demographic dividend — large working-age population entering peak earning

blocks.content: Six themes underpinning the investment view.

items: ARR Transformation: ARR at 65% of net revenues (up from 61%); fee-based at 45%. Annuity income reduces transaction-cycle volatility.
Profitability Momentum: Q2FY25 PAT INR 1,242 Cr (+123% YoY); H1FY26 operating PAT INR 1,088 Cr (+11% YoY); Asset & PWM businesses leading at +32% PAT growth.
AUM Scale and Outperformance: Total AUM INR 1.77L Cr (+46% YoY). AMC AUM INR 1.89L Cr (+33%) with INR 11,610 Cr net flows. 91% of AUM outperforming benchmarks over 3 years.
Private Wealth Expansion: PWM AUM INR 1.96L Cr (+31% YoY); family count +41% YoY; PAT +14% (9M). Distribution book +34% YoY to INR 42,775 Cr.
Valuation Opportunity: CMP INR 822.90 vs consensus TP INR 1,126.67 (max INR 1,200) — 37% upside. Probability-weighted target INR 1,109.
Credit Upgrade: Long-term credit rating upgraded to AA+ (Stable) by ICRA. Net worth INR 12,871 Cr (+16% YoY).

blocks.content: Revenue trajectory across the forecast period reflects mix shift and AUM scale-up.
Recent quarterly trajectory showing the variability inherent to the cyclical revenue base.
Margin and return profile across the forecast period.
Probability-weighted target price reconciles bull, base, and bear cases.

- ARR contribution crossing 70% of revenues, triggering valuation re-rating from broking to asset management multiples
- Sustained AMC AUM growth above 25% YoY with net flows exceeding INR 10,000 crore annually
- Private wealth AUM growth above 25% YoY with family count expansion
- Operating leverage improvement with cost-to-income ratio declining
- Regulatory clarity on F&O trading norms

items: ARR contribution crossing 70% of revenues, triggering valuation re-rating from broking to asset management multiples
Sustained AMC AUM growth above 25% YoY with net flows exceeding INR 10,000 crore annually
Private wealth AUM growth above 25% YoY with family count expansion
Operating leverage improvement with cost-to-income ratio declining
Regulatory clarity on F&O trading norms

blocks.content: Founder-led leadership with deep institutional DNA.

Recurring regulatory matters are operational rather than fraudulent. Despite SEBI interventions, structural lapses suggest deeper governance attention needed in risk management. Pattern warrants monitoring but does not impair the business model.

Entry: INR 800-850. 15-20% discount to analyst consensus target — margin of safety.
Position size: 3-5% of portfolio (institutional).
Review: Quarterly.
Downside exit: Stop-loss at INR 750 (-8% from CMP) on technical break with high volume.
Upside exit: Sell 50% at INR 1,100-1,150 (30-40% gains) / Sell remaining at INR 1,200+ if multiples expand beyond 25x P/E.

items: ARR contribution above 60% of net revenues
AMC AUM growth above 25% YoY with net flows > INR 10,000 Cr
PWM AUM growth above 25% YoY with family count expansion
Cost-to-income ratio declining
SEBI compliance with no major enforcement actions
SEBI regulatory headwinds on F&O trading norms compressing cyclical broking revenues
Q2FY26 profit declined 67.6% YoY and revenue 34.9% YoY due to regulatory changes — residual cyclicality
Competitive intensity from discount brokers and fintech platforms compressing yields
Forensic / governance: recurring SEBI matters require monitoring (Monitor Closely category)
Mark-to-market volatility on the proprietary book
Housing finance asset-quality slippage in stressed credit cycles
